one_line_thesis: Decentralized VMS acquirer reinvesting FCFA2S at hurdle rates — base 16.0% IRR supports core stance; GAAP net income secondary to FCFA2S compounding.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Constellation is the reference serial acquirer of VMS: decentralized business units, Mark Leonard culture, and 100% of FCFA2S reinvested at disciplined hurdles. Filing extracts confirm Q1 2026 acceleration ($3.18B revenue, +20%) and FY2025 scale ($11.62B, total assets $16.17B, +26% YoY). At C$2,780/sh (Yahoo CSU.TO close 2026-07-20), base annual return is 12.08% per year (bear 4.3%, bull 15.6%). Stable moat from hundreds of micro-franchises; partial dhando (premium multiple, M&A price inflation). Marvin proposes watch on updated spot math; approved stance core remains the incumbent allocato...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Complete isolated rater outputs, proposer case, pre-mortem, and research response without sharing peer votes.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Complete isolated rater outputs, proposer case, pre-mortem, and research response without sharing peer votes.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Vertical-market software owner-cash engine
components.cross_check: Reconcile the range to primary filings, a reproducible owner-cash or NAV bridge, and the identified liability and capital claims before decision use.

components.label: Acquisition reinvestment runway
components.cross_check: Reconcile the range to primary filings, a reproducible owner-cash or NAV bridge, and the identified liability and capital claims before decision use.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Existing research and filing-derived valuation inputs. The primary-source reconciliation remains an open evidence gate.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of CSU, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.unit_source: Derived from inputs.shares_millions in the pre-existing valuation.json; [HUMAN REVIEW] confirm against the latest primary filing.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

valuation_proof.evidence: Primary bridge from CSU/official-reports/Q4-2025-Shareholder-Report.pdf: FY2025 FCFA2S $1683.0M, diluted shares 21.19153M, cash $3089M, recourse debt $1489M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation_proof.adjustment: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key core_engine; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows that the economic claim, cash conversion, ownership, or required capital is materially worse than this low case.

timeline.label: Q1 2026 revenue $3.18B (+20% YoY); organic growth disclosed in shareholder letter
Continued 100% FCFA2S reinvestment into VMS acquisitions at hurdle rates
Lumine preferred share investment and Topicus orbit expansion remain capital-allocation themes
Mark Leonard culture and decentralized GP model remain allocator backbone
Constellation IPO and serial acquirer model established on TSX

industry.structure: Vertical market software is fragmented across hundreds of mission-critical niches; Constellation aggregates via decentralized business units with maintenance-heavy recurring revenue and high switching costs.
industry.share_shift: Constellation continues consolidating VMS globally; competition from Tyler, Roper, and PE strategics inflates acquisition multiples but CSU scale still finds hurdle-rate deals.
industry.trend: Maintenance/recurring mix rising; organic growth mid-single digits at consolidated scale; earnout fair-value marks distort GAAP earnings while FCFA2S remains the owner metric.

top_claims.claim: Constellation is the reference serial acquirer of VMS: decentralized business units, Mark Leonard culture, and 100% of FCFA2S reinvested at disciplined hurdles. Filing extracts confirm Q1 2026 acceleration ($3.18B revenue, +20%) and FY2025 scale ($11.62B, total assets $16.17B, +26% YoY). At ~$1,933 USD/sh (CSU.TO C$2,6
We expect **18.73%** per year over 7 years at **~$1932.74** on **$79.5/sh** starting owner cash.
*Primary risk: M&A multiple inflation erodes reinvestment returns as CSU scale makes hurdle-rate deals harder to find.
Earnout / fair-value P&L volatility distracting from FCFA2S (Lumine preferred investment revaluation line in Q4 extract).

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: M&A multiple inflation erodes reinvestment returns as CSU scale makes hurdle-rate deals harder to find.
Earnout / fair-value P&L volatility distracting from FCFA2S (Lumine preferred investment revaluation line in Q4 extract).
Key person (Mark Leonard) as culture carrier.
